heading: Grant Access to MongoDB Support Only for Database Logs
body:
If an issue that requires MongoDB Support arises and you want to allow
MongoDB support staff limited-time access only to database logs within
your organization, you can do so with the following procedure.**Api:**Atlas Administration APITo grant access to MongoDB support staff to database logs through
the Atlas Administration API, see [Grant MongoDB employee cluster access for one cluster](api/doc/atlas-admin-api-v2/operation/operation-grantgroupclustermongodbemployeeaccess).You can specify `CLUSTER_DATABASE_LOGS` for the `grantType` field in the request body schema.**Ui:**Atlas UI- Select General Settings from the Settings menu in the navigation bar.

- Toggle Block MongoDB Support Access to Atlas Cluster Infrastructure and Logs to `Yes`.* Select the ellipsis icon (...) to the right of the cluster.

* Select Grant Temporary Infrastructure Access to MongoDB Support.

* Select Only database logs.

* Click Grant Access.

  IMPORTANT: You can grant MongoDB Support staff access only to database logs for 24 hours.
  Any downloads in progress won't be interrupted if you revoke this access. Database audit logs are
  not included in this access grant.
